Mohammad Rayedul Islam
About

The Science
of Matter

As a Materials Science and Engineering student, I am deeply engaged in research and inquiry, with strong interests in the fundamental principles that govern structure–property–processing relationships. I am particularly focused on advanced functional materials, including quantum materials, semiconductors, nanomaterials, and biomaterials.

My research interests also extend to density functional theory (DFT) and computational materials science, which I consider powerful tools to investigate and predict material behavior at the atomic scale. I am motivated to advance innovation in materials design, performance, and sustainability through a rigorous combination of theory, computation, and application-driven research.

Undergraduate Research

Experimental
Experience

Hands-on laboratory training at BUET spanning characterization, mechanical testing, and thermal processing of metallic and ceramic systems. Every experiment bridges theory with observable material behavior.

🔬
Optical Microscopy
Microstructural observation of metallic alloys, grain boundary imaging, phase identification
🔥
Heat Treatment
Annealing, quenching, tempering — studying phase transformations and microstructure evolution
💎
Hardness Testing
Vickers & Rockwell hardness measurement; correlation with microstructure and treatment history
⚡
XRD Analysis
X-ray diffraction for phase identification, crystal structure determination, and lattice parameter calculation
🧲
Tensile Testing
Uniaxial stress-strain characterization; yield strength, UTS, ductility, and elastic modulus determination

Academic Projects

Selected Work

01
May 2025 – Jul 2025
Sustainable Drainage System Improvement Plan for BUET Area
MME 202 Group 02 · Level 1 Term 1 BUET

Led a comprehensive project developing an engineered drainage improvement strategy for the BUET campus under formal academic supervision. Addressed root causes including undersized pipes, hydraulic mismatch, flat terrain slope, and inadequate sewage disposal. Applied sustainable material selection, GIS hydrologic modelling (HEC-RAS), risk identification, and multi-criteria project monitoring methodologies — delivering a rigorous 60-crore BDT infrastructure-grade proposal with a full 10-month Gantt timeline.

Project Management Hydraulic Design Sustainable Materials GIS · HEC-RAS BUET
↓  Download Report
02
Oct 2024 – Nov 2024
Journey of Biomaterials: An Emerging Field in Material Science
HUM 102 Level 1 · Term 2 · Section A-1 BUET

Academic research report completed under the Department of Humanities, BUET. A structured inquiry into the evolution, classification, and frontier applications of biomaterials — spanning metallic, ceramic, polymeric, and composite biomedical systems. Covered medical applications (orthopedics, tissue engineering, cardiovascular implants, drug delivery), environmental uses, innovative synthesis techniques including 3D printing and genetic engineering, and future trends in smart biomaterials, nanotechnology, and AI-driven research.

Biomaterials Tissue Engineering Nanotechnology Literature Review BUET
↓  Download Report

Experience

Professional Roles

Nov 2023 – Present 2 yrs 5 mos · Full-time
Dhaka, Bangladesh · On-site
Team Coordinator
Material Advantage BUET Chapter

Material Advantage BUET Chapter is part of the global Material Advantage program, providing student access to four of the world's leading materials science and engineering societies: ACerS, AIST, ASM International, and TMS. As Team Coordinator, I drive chapter operations, coordinate with international society networks, and represent student interests in the global materials community.

Activities: Organizing seminars and workshops · Society networking · Event coordination · Team management
